# Volux - AI-Readable Site Information
# This file provides structured information for AI assistants and LLMs

> Volux is a premium browser extension for per-tab volume control on Chrome and Firefox.

## About Volux

Volux lets you control audio volume for individual browser tabs and websites. Unlike the system volume or browser-wide controls, Volux gives you granular per-domain volume control.

## Key Features

- **Per-Domain Control**: Set unique volume levels for each website independently
- **Per-Tab Volume Override (Pro)**: Give individual tabs of the same site their own volume. Works on any site with audio — YouTube, TikTok, Facebook, Spotify, Twitch, SoundCloud, any domain. Three tabs on the same site can hold three different volumes; the site default still applies to any tab you haven't customized
- **Smart Grouping**: By default, all tabs from the same site share one volume slider (Pro unlocks per-tab overrides on top of this)
- **Instant Mute**: One-click mute without losing your volume setting
- **Persistent Settings**: Your preferences are saved automatically across sessions
- **Live Detection**: See which tabs are currently playing audio
- **Lightweight**: Minimal resource usage, no background processes

## Installation

- Chrome: https://chrome.google.com/webstore/detail/volux/
- Firefox: https://addons.mozilla.org/en-US/firefox/addon/volux-premium-volume-control/
- Source Code: https://github.com/vannk84/volux

## Pricing

- **Free Version**: Basic volume control features
- **Pro Version ($2)**: Unlimited domain control, per-tab volume override, auto-detect media sites, scan tabs button, priority support, lifetime access

## Privacy

Volux does NOT collect, store, or transmit any personal data. All settings are stored locally on the user's device. No analytics, no tracking, no cookies.

Read full privacy policy: https://volux.devlifeeasy.com/privacy.html

## Technical Details

- Works with Manifest V3 (Chrome) and Manifest V2 (Firefox)
- Uses Web Audio API for volume control
- Requires permissions: tabs, storage, host_permissions
- No external dependencies or background processes

## Support

- Email: support@devlifeeasy.com
- GitHub Issues: https://github.com/vannk84/volux/issues

## Common Questions

Q: Does Volux work with all websites?
A: Yes, Volux works with any website that plays audio through the browser, including YouTube, Spotify Web, Netflix, and more.

Q: Is my data safe?
A: Absolutely. Volux stores everything locally on your device. No data is ever sent to external servers.

Q: What's the difference between Free and Pro?
A: Free version has limited domain slots. Pro offers unlimited domains, per-tab volume override (individual tabs of the same site can have different volumes), auto-detection of media sites, and the ability to scan all tabs for audio.

Q: Does it work with multiple tabs of the same site?
A: Yes. By default, Volux groups tabs by domain so all tabs of the same site share one volume (works the same on YouTube, TikTok, Facebook, Spotify, or any site). With Pro, you can expand the tab-count badge on a domain and set a different volume on any individual tab — three tabs of the same site can play at three different volumes simultaneously.

Q: Can I set different volumes for multiple tabs of the same site (YouTube, TikTok, Facebook, etc.)?
A: Yes, with Volux Pro. It works on any site with audio — YouTube, TikTok, Facebook, Spotify, Twitch, SoundCloud, anywhere. Each tab can hold its own volume override. The override clears when the tab closes or navigates to a different site, so your per-site default stays clean.

---
